skill.name: minimalist-ui
skill.description: Clean editorial-style interfaces. Warm monochrome palette, typographic contrast, flat bento grids, muted pastels. No gradients, no heavy shadows.
skill.summary: Generate premium minimalist web interfaces with warm monochrome palettes and editorial typography
skill.author: leonxlnx

security_audit.summary: All 102 static findings are false positives triggered by CSS font-family declarations and UI design terminology. The SKILL.md is a legitimate design specification document. No actual shell commands, cryptographic code, keyloggers, or network exfiltration patterns exist. The hardcoded URL is a standard placeholder image service (picsum.photos) commonly used in web development.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: CSS Font Declarations Flagged as Shell Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer incorrectly flagged 84 instances of backtick characters in CSS font-family declarations as shell command execution. In this document, backticks appear in font stack syntax like `'Geist Mono', 'SF Mono', 'JetBrains Mono'`. This is standard CSS syntax with no shell execution risk.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed the entire file. All backticks appear in CSS font-family declarations, not shell commands.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: UI Design Term Flagged as Keylogger
dangerous_patterns.description: The word 'keystroke' in lines 28 and 58 refers to rendering keyboard shortcuts in UI (using <kbd> tags). This is legitimate UI design terminology for displaying keyboard shortcuts, not keylogging functionality.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Context shows these are within sections about rendering keyboard shortcuts visually, not actual keylogging code.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: CSS Properties Flagged as Cryptographic Algorithms
dangerous_patterns.description: CSS property names and font references triggered 'weak cryptographic algorithm' flags. No cryptographic code exists in this document.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: CSS hex color codes (#EAEAEA) and font names triggered this pattern match. No crypto algorithms present.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Picsum.photos URL Flagged as Hardcoded External Resource
dangerous_patterns.description: The URL https://picsum.photos/seed/{context}/1200/800 is a standard placeholder image service used in web development. This is legitimate and not a security concern.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: picsum.photos is a well-known placeholder image service commonly used in web development templates and documentation.

content.value_statement: Most AI-generated interfaces look generic and derivative. This skill guides Claude to produce refined, editorial-style web designs with warm monochrome palettes, typographic hierarchy, and premium aesthetics.
actual_capabilities: Generate clean, editorial-style web interfaces with warm monochrome palettes
Apply typographic hierarchy using serif headings and sans-serif body text
Create bento-grid layouts with 1px borders and generous whitespace
Design subtle hover states and scroll-entry animations
Implement muted pastel color accents for tags and status indicators
Avoid common design anti-patterns like rounded pills, heavy shadows, and generic fonts
limitations: Does not generate actual images or photography assets
Output quality depends on the user's frontend framework and CSS expertise
Cannot guarantee pixel-perfect rendering across all browsers
CSS output assumes Tailwind CSS or similar utility framework

use_cases.title: Startup Landing Pages
use_cases.description: Create sophisticated, minimalist landing pages that stand out from typical SaaS designs.
use_cases.target_user: Founders and marketers building product sites

use_cases.title: Documentation Sites
use_cases.description: Generate clean, readable documentation layouts with editorial typography.
use_cases.target_user: Technical writers and developer relations teams

use_cases.title: Design System Components
use_cases.description: Build consistent UI components following minimalist design principles.

prompt_templates.title: Landing Page Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a minimalist landing page for [PRODUCT NAME]. Use the Premium Utilitarian Minimalism style with warm monochrome palette, editorial serif headings, and bento-grid feature sections. Include a hero section, three feature cards, and a pricing table.
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants a clean, high-end landing page

prompt_templates.title: UI Component Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create the following UI components using minimal design principles: (1) Primary CTA button with #111111 background, (2) Tag badges with muted pastel backgrounds, (3) Accordion FAQ section with only 1px bottom borders. Use the defined color palette and typography.
prompt_templates.scenario: When user needs specific UI components

prompt_templates.title: Pricing Table Layout
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a pricing table with three tiers using bento-grid layout. Each tier should have generous padding (24px-40px), 1px #EAEAEA borders, and crisp 8px border-radius. Use off-black text (#2F3437) and muted gray secondary text (#787774).
prompt_templates.scenario: When user needs a pricing section

prompt_templates.title: Full Landing Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a complete landing page for [PRODUCT TYPE]. Follow the Premium Utilitarian Minimalism protocol: warm monochrome palette (#FFFFFF, #F7F6F3, #111111), editorial serif headings (Lyon Text/Newsreader), system sans-serif body (SF Pro/Geist). Include: hero with tight-tracking headlines, bento feature grid, testimonials section, and sticky navbar with minimal blur. Add scroll-entry animations with translateY(12px) fade-in.
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants a complete page

output_examples.input: Create a minimalist pricing section with three tiers
output_examples.output: A pricing section with three cards in a bento grid layout. Each card has 1px #EAEAEA borders, generous 32px padding, crisp 8px border-radius. Primary tier has #111111 CTA button. Secondary text uses #787774. No shadows, no gradients.

An accordion with no container boxes. Each item separated by 1px #EAEAEA bottom border. Clean + and - toggles using Radix or Phosphor icons. Off-black text (#2F3437) with 1.6 line-height for readability.

output_examples.input: Create a hero section for a SaaS product
output_examples.output: A hero with editorial serif heading using Playfair Display or Newsreader, tight -0.02em tracking, 1.1 line-height. Warm off-white background (#FBFBFA). Subtitle in Switzer or SF Pro at generous line-height. Optional ambient radial gradient at 0.03 opacity.

best_practices: Start by defining the color palette and typography hierarchy before adding components
Use generous whitespace (py-24 or py-32 between sections) to establish premium feel
Constrain content width to max-w-4xl or max-w-5xl for optimal readability
Apply scroll-entry animations using IntersectionObserver for subtle, performant reveals
anti_patterns: Using generic fonts like Inter, Roboto, or Open Sans which feel corporate and derivative
Applying heavy Tailwind shadows (shadow-md, shadow-lg) that contradict the minimalist aesthetic
Using bright primary colors, gradients, or glassmorphism effects that break the monochrome palette
Adding rounded-full pill shapes to large containers or primary buttons

faq.question: What frameworks does this skill work with?
faq.answer: The design principles work with any CSS framework. Output examples use Tailwind CSS syntax, but the design rules can be adapted for CSS modules, styled-components, or plain CSS.

faq.question: Does this skill generate actual images?
faq.answer: No. The skill generates CSS and HTML markup. It references picsum.photos for placeholder images and describes illustration styles, but does not generate image files.

faq.question: Can I use this for mobile-responsive designs?
faq.answer: Yes. The design principles include responsive behavior. Bento grids collapse to single columns on mobile, typography scales appropriately, and whitespace adjusts proportionally.

faq.question: What makes this different from generic AI design output?
faq.answer: This skill enforces specific constraints that prevent generic output: premium font selections, warm monochrome palettes, 1px borders, generous whitespace, and editorial typography hierarchy. It actively rejects common anti-patterns.

faq.question: How do I customize the color palette?
faq.answer: The base palette uses warm bone (#F7F6F3), off-black text (#2F3437), and ultra-light borders (#EAEAEA). You can adjust these values while maintaining the warm monochrome aesthetic and contrast ratios.

faq.question: What animation performance considerations apply?
faq.answer: All animations use only transform and opacity properties to avoid layout thrashing. Use will-change sparingly, prefer cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1) for smooth easing, and implement scroll animations via IntersectionObserver, not scroll event listeners.
